title: 16 PF Personality Assessment Report: Peter Kastner
author: 16 PF Assessment (OPP Ltd.)
abstract: A formal 16 Personality Factors (16PF) psychometric assessment conducted on Peter S. Kastner dated 17 July 2001. The report evaluates Kastner across four domains: interpersonal relationships, coping with pressure, decision making and problem solving, and team behaviour, characterizing him as an extravert, resilient coordinator-type with strong social facility, dominant assertiveness, practical rather than abstract thinking, and a preference for collaborative decision-making.
importance_rationale: Provides a formal third-party psychological profile of a prominent IT industry analyst at the peak of his Aberdeen Group career, offering rare structured insight into analyst personality and professional style.
relevance_rationale: The 16PF dimensions assessed remain standard in professional psychology; the profile illuminates the personal traits behind Kastner's analyst methodology and communication style.
prescience_rationale: Psychometric assessment document; no forward-looking predictions made.
